summary: Toonflow (v1.1.8, by HBAI-Ltd, Apache-2.0 + commercial rider) is an "AI short-drama factory": a desktop tool that turns a novel into a finished short-form video series through a staged pipeline â€” novel import â†’ per-chapter event extraction â†’ ScriptAgent (story skeleton â†’ adaptation strategy â†’ per-episode screenplay) â†’ asset extraction (characters/scenes/props) â†’ AI image generation in a chosen art style â†’ ProductionAgent (director plan â†’ derived-asset analysis/generation â†’ storyboard table â†’ storyboard panel â†’ storyboard images) â†’ video workbench (per-track video-prompt generation tuned per video model, multi-reference video generation, candidate selection) â†’ in-frontend stitching/export. It is an Electron 40 shell around an Express 5 + Socket.IO + better-sqlite3 backend; the React frontend lives in a separate repo (Toonflow-web) and ships precompiled in data/web. All LLM/image/video calls go through user-editable "vendor" TypeScript files executed in a vm2 sandbox on top of the Vercel AI SDK, and all agent prompts are externalized as editable Markdown "skill" files. UI and prompts are Chinese-first with 7 UI locales.
architecture:
PROCESS TOPOLOGY
- Electron main (scripts/main.ts): on first run/version bump copies bundled data/ (assets, models, serve, skills, web, vendor) into userData/data; require()s the compiled server (data/serve/app.js) in-process and starts it on a random port; loads data/web/index.html into a frameless BrowserWindow; registers a custom `toonflow://` protocol the renderer fetches for window controls (minimize/maximize/close/restart), getAppUrl (API base), openDevTools, open-in-browser, and OS locale.
- Headless mode: `src/app.ts` runs standalone on port 10588 (Docker/PM2). Env: NODE_ENV, PORT, OSSURL.

BACKEND (src/)
- Express 5 with convention-based routing: src/core.ts globs src/routes/**/*.ts and code-generates src/router.ts so each file mounts at /api/<path> (e.g. routes/script/extractAssets.ts â†’ POST /api/script/extractAssets). Every route file exports an express.Router with zod `validateFields` middleware; responses use {code,message,data} helpers (lib/responseFormat).
- Auth: POST /api/login/login checks o_user (seeded admin/admin123, plaintext), signs a 180-day JWT with a per-install random 8-char key stored in o_setting.tokenKey; a global middleware verifies Authorization header or ?token= for all other /api routes. Socket.IO namespaces verify the same JWT from handshake.auth.token.
- Static serving: /oss â†’ data/oss (local "object storage"; `?size=200x300` or `?size=30%` triggers on-demand sharp thumbnails cached under data/oss/smallImage), /skills (image files only), /assets, and the SPA from data/web.
- DB: knex + better-sqlite3 at data/db2.sqlite. lib/initDB.ts declaratively creates 25 tables and seeds defaults (admin user, agent deploy rows, settings, prompt templates, vendor rows, skill list with ONNX embeddings); lib/fixDB.ts holds migrations; in dev, @rmp135/sql-ts regenerates src/types/database.d.ts from the live schema.
- AI layer (utils/ai.ts): u.Ai.Text(key, think?, thinkLevel?)/.Image(key)/.Video(key)/.Audio(key). `key` is either an agent role key (e.g. "scriptAgent:decisionAgent") resolved through o_agentDeploy + o_setting.agentUseMode ("0"=simple: subagents inherit the parent agent's model; "1"=advanced: per-subagent model), or a literal "vendorId:modelName". Text wraps Vercel AI SDK generateText/streamText with stopWhen stepCountIs(toolCount*50), per-agent temperature/maxOutputTokens, extractReasoningMiddleware(tagName "reasoning_content"), and optional @ai-sdk/devtools middleware (setting switchAiDevTool). Image/Video/Audio call the vendor's imageRequest/videoRequest/ttsRequest, convert returned URLs to base64, and `.save(path)` writes into OSS. Every generation can be wrapped in withTaskRecord which inserts an o_tasks row ("in progress") and closes it as completed/failed with reason.
- Programmable vendor system (utils/vendor.ts + utils/vm.ts + data/vendor/*.ts): each provider is a TS file (toonflow, openai, deepseek, atlascloud, volcengine, minimax, klingai, vidu, grsai, volcengineSd2...) editable from the settings UI at runtime; compiled with sucrase, run inside vm2 with an injected sandbox: AI-SDK factories (createOpenAI/Anthropic/Google/DeepSeek/Zhipu/Qwen/Xai/Minimax/OpenAICompatible), axios, fetch, FormData, jsonwebtoken, crypto, logger, plus helpers zipImage (compress base64 to byte budget), zipImageResolution, mergeImages (horizontal montage with size cap), urlToBase64, and pollTask(fn, interval=3s, timeout=50min) for async video job polling. Vendor exports: `vendor` config (id, version, inputs/inputValues for apiKey+baseUrl, models[] with per-model type/mode/audio/durationResolutionMap/think) and textRequest/imageRequest/videoRequest/ttsRequest adapters. Model lists merge code-declared models with user-added ones (o_vendorConfig.models JSON).
- Agents (src/agents/{scriptAgent,productionAgent}): three-layer architecture. The decision layer is the only chat-facing model; it gets a system prompt read from data/skills/*_agent_decision.md, an assistant message carrying project info + memory context, and tools = memory.deepRetrieve + read tools + run_sub_agent_* tools. Each run_sub_agent_* spawns a fresh streamText run with its own skill-file system prompt and a â‰¤100-char task brief, streams into a new chat message bubble (named "ç¼–å‰§/Screenwriter", "æ‰§è¡Œå¯¼æ¼”/Executive Director", "ç¼–è¾‘/Editor", "ç›‘åˆ¶/Supervising Producer"), and stores its XML-stripped output into memory. Subagent output containing workspace XML (<storySkeleton>, <adaptationStrategy>, <scriptItem>, <scriptPlan>, <storyboardTable>) is parsed by the FRONTEND from the streamed text and written into the workspace panes.
- Socket protocol (src/socket): namespaces /api/socket/scriptAgent and /api/socket/productionAgent. Clientâ†’server: chat{content}, stop, updateThinkConfig{think, thinkLevel 0-3}, updateContext{isolationKey, projectId, scriptId}. Serverâ†’client: message{id,role,name,status,datetime,content[]}, message:update{status}, content:add, content:update{strategy:append|merge} with content types text/markdown/thinking/search/image/suggestion/toolcall/activity/reasoning (ResTool/MessageBuilder builders; AutoThinkingTextStream converts inline <think>â€¦</think> into thinking blocks with elapsed-time titles). Crucially, agent TOOLS call back into the renderer with socket.emit+ack: getPlanData/getFlowData read current workspace state held by the renderer; addDeriveAsset/delDeriveAsset write DB then notify the canvas; addStoryboard, generateDeriveAsset, generateStoryboard ask the renderer to add nodes / fire the REST generation endpoints. A serial socket queue (800 ms spacing) prevents flooding the renderer.
- Memory (utils/agent/memory.ts + embedding.ts): table `memories` keyed by isolationKey (per project/episode chat thread). add() stores message + local embedding (all-MiniLM-L6-v2 fp16 ONNX via @huggingface/transformers, fully offline); every messagesPerSummary (default 10, configurable in o_setting) unsummarized messages are compressed by the agent's own LLM into a â‰¤summaryMaxLength summary row linked to source ids. get(query) returns shortTerm (recent unsummarized), summaries (recent N), and rag (cosine top-k over all messages), rendered into a "## Memory" assistant context block ("[Related memories] / [History summaries] / [Recent conversation]"). deepRetrieve(keyword) = vector-search summaries â†’ LLM picks relevant summary ids (JSON array) â†’ expand to original messages; exposed as an agent tool.
- Skill system (utils/agent/skillsTools.ts): markdown files with YAML frontmatter (name/description); buildSkillPrompt injects an <available_skills> list; activate_skill loads full body once (dedup), read_skill_file reads bundled resources with path-traversal guards; per-project art style (data/skills/art_skills/<style>/driector_skills/*.md), story genre (data/skills/story_skills/<genre>/driector_skills/*.md) and production technique skills (production_skills/*.md) are offered to production subagents. Settings UI lists/edits all skill files (o_skillList tracks md5/description/embedding/state).
- Generation task management: no job queue framework. Long jobs are fire-and-forget after an immediate HTTP response; state machines live in DB columns (Chinese literals: æœªç”Ÿæˆ/ç”Ÿæˆä¸­/å·²å®Œæˆ/ç”ŸæˆæˆåŠŸ/ç”Ÿæˆå¤±è´¥ = not generated/generating/completed/success/failed, plus eventState/extractState ints 0 in-progress, 1 done, -1 failed, 2 queued) with errorReason/reason columns; the frontend polls dedicated endpoints (assets/pollingImageAssets, assets/pollingPromptAssets, production/storyboard/pollingImage, workbench/checkVideoStateList, cornerScape/pollingAudio, novel/getNovelEventState, script/pollScriptAssets) that return only rows whose state left "generating". Concurrency is hand-rolled: cleanNovel uses a worker-pool of N (default 5); storyboard batchGenerateImage runs batches of concurrentCount (default 5); extractAssets groups 5 scripts per LLM call. Retries: axios-retry imported; urlToBase64 retries 3x with linear backoff; vendor pollTask handles provider-side async jobs.
domainModel:
All tables prefixed o_ except `memories`. Integer PKs are mostly knex auto-increment unless noted.

- o_user: id, name, password (plaintext). Single seeded admin.
- o_project: id (= Date.now()), projectType, name, intro (synopsis), type (genre), artStyle (folder name of art skill, e.g. "2D_90s_japanese_anime"), directorManual (story-genre skill folder, e.g. "Sweet_romance_novel"), mode (video model mode: string like "startFrameOptional" OR JSON array like ["imageReference:9","videoReference:3","audioReference:3"] â€” array â‡’ "multi-reference" project), videoRatio ("16:9"/"9:16"), imageModel & videoModel ("vendorId:modelName"), imageQuality ("1K"/"2K"/"4K"), createTime, userId.
- o_artStyle: custom user art styles â€” id, name, fileUrl, label, prompt (style prompt extracted by AI from reference images).
- o_agentDeploy: per-agent model bindings â€” key ("scriptAgent","productionAgent","universalAi","ttsDubbing", plus subagent keys "scriptAgent:decisionAgent|supervisionAgent|storySkeletonAgent|adaptationStrategyAgent|scriptAgent", "productionAgent:decisionAgent|supervisionAgent|deriveAssetsAgent|generateAssetsAgent|directorPlanAgent|storyboardGenAgent|storyboardPanelAgent|storyboardTableAgent"), model, modelName ("vendorId:model"), vendorId, name/desc (Chinese display), temperature, maxOutputTokens, disabled.
- o_setting: key/value â€” tokenKey, agentUseMode, memory tuning (messagesPerSummary=10, shortTermLimit=5, summaryMaxLength=500, summaryLimit=10, ragLimit=3, deepRetrieveSummaryLimit=5), modelOnnxFile, modelDtype, switchAiDevTool.
- o_tasks (task center): id, projectId, taskClass ("è§’è‰²å›¾ç”Ÿæˆ/åœºæ™¯å›¾ç”Ÿæˆ/é“å…·å›¾ç”Ÿæˆ/ç”Ÿæˆåˆ†é•œå›¾ç‰‡/è§†é¢‘ç”Ÿæˆ/å·¥ä½œæµå›¾ç‰‡ç”Ÿæˆâ€¦" = role/scene/prop image gen, storyboard image gen, video gen, workflow image gen), relatedObjects (JSON), model, describe, state (è¿›è¡Œä¸­/å·²å®Œæˆ/ç”Ÿæˆå¤±è´¥), startTime, reason.
- o_prompt: editable global prompt templates â€” name, type ("eventExtraction", "scriptAssetExtraction", "videoPromptGeneration", "audioBindPrompt"), data (default), useData (user override wins).
- o_modelPrompt: binds a video model to a specific prompt md file â€” vendorId, model, fileName, path (under data/modelPrompt).
- o_novel: one row per chapter â€” id, chapterIndex (1-based per project), reel (volume), chapter (title), chapterData (raw text), projectId, eventState (0 pending/in-progress, 1 ok, -1 failed), event (extracted single-line event summary), errorReason, createTime.
- o_event / o_eventChapter: standalone event entities + eventâ†”chapter join (lightly used; main event store is o_novel.event).
- o_script: an episode screenplay â€” id, name ("Work EP01: title"), content (markdown screenplay), projectId, extractState (asset extraction: 2 queued, 0 extracting, 1 done, -1 failed), errorReason, createTime.
- o_assets: unified asset table â€” id, name, prompt (image-gen prompt), remark, type ("role"|"scene"|"tool"|"clip"|"audio"), describe (visual description), scriptId, imageIdâ†’o_image (current image), assetsId (SELF-REFERENCE: non-null â‡’ this is a DERIVED asset/state-variant of parent asset), projectId, flowId (image-flow editor link), startTime, promptState (prompt polish: ç”Ÿæˆä¸­/å·²å®Œæˆ/å¤±è´¥), audioBindState, promptErrorReason. Derived assets: characters get only "transformation" variants (outfit change/transformation VFX/shapeshift), scenes only time-of-day variants, props never.
- o_image: generated image versions â€” id, filePath (OSS path), type, assetsId, model, resolution, state (ç”Ÿæˆä¸­/å·²å®Œæˆ/ç”Ÿæˆå¤±è´¥), errorReason. An asset keeps history; o_assets.imageId points at the active one.
- o_storyboard: one shot/panel â€” id, scriptId, prompt (image prompt; null in pure-text multi-ref mode), filePath (generated frame), duration (sec), state, trackIdâ†’o_videoTrack (auto-created per storyboard), reason, track (group label, sequential "1","2"â€¦), videoDesc (structured Chinese description: picture description, scene, linked asset names, duration, shot size, camera move, character action, emotion, lighting/mood, dialogue, SFX, linked asset ids), shouldGenerateImage (0/1), projectId, flowId, index (sort order), createTime.
- o_assets2Storyboard: ordered join storyboardIdâ†”assetId (rowid order = reference-image order = @å›¾N numbering).
- o_scriptAssets: join scriptIdâ†”assetId (which episode uses which asset).
- o_agentWorkData: serialized workspace JSON â€” projectId, episodesId, key ("scriptAgent" project-level: {storySkeleton, adaptationStrategy}; "productionAgent" per-episode: FlowData {script, scriptPlan, assets, storyboardTable, storyboard}), createTime/updateTime. getFlowData rehydrates assets/storyboard live from DB and overlays them on the stored JSON.
- o_video: one generated video candidate â€” id, filePath, errorReason, time, state (ç”Ÿæˆä¸­/ç”ŸæˆæˆåŠŸ/ç”Ÿæˆå¤±è´¥), scriptId, projectId, videoTrackId.
- o_videoTrack: a workbench track/segment â€” id (= Date.now()), videoId (selected candidate), projectId, scriptId, state (video-prompt generation state), reason, prompt (generated video prompt text), selectVideoId, duration.
- o_vendorConfig: id (vendor name), inputValues JSON (apiKey/baseUrlâ€¦), models JSON (user-added models), enable 0/1. Code lives in data/vendor/<id>.ts.
- o_imageFlow: node-graph editor persistence â€” flowData JSON {nodes, edges}; node types "upload" (image) and "generated" (generatedImage + references[]).
- o_skillList / o_skillAttribution: registry of skill md files (id=md5 of path, md5 of content, path, name, description, embedding JSON, type main|references, state) and which agent file each reference skill belongs to.
- memories: id (uuid), isolationKey, type message|summary, role (user / assistant:decision / assistant:execution[:stage] / assistant:supervision), name, content, embedding JSON, relatedMessageIds JSON (summaryâ†’messages), summarized 0/1, createTime.
- o_assetsRole2Audio: role-asset â†” audio-asset voice binding.

featureInventory.howItWorks: POST /api/project/addProject with projectType, name, intro, type(genre), artStyle (one of 11 bundled art-skill folders or custom o_artStyle), directorManual (one of 12 story-genre skill folders), videoRatio (16:9/9:16), imageModel/videoModel ('vendorId:model' picked from /api/modelSelect/getModelList which aggregates enabled vendors), imageQuality (1K/2K/4K), mode (the chosen video model's mode â€” string for first/last-frame models or JSON array for multi-reference models like Seedance 2.0). mode being an array drives the entire downstream branching ('å¤šå‚/multi-reference: yes/no').

featureInventory.feature: Novel import with AI-assisted chapter splitting
featureInventory.howItWorks: Frontend pastes raw novel text; POST /api/script/getAiRegex asks universalAi to return a JS regex (two capture groups: episode number incl. Chinese numerals + title) detecting the chapter delimiter pattern from the first 2000 chars; frontend splits and POSTs /api/novel/addNovel [{index, reel, chapter, chapterData}]; chapterIndex auto-continues from the project's max; event extraction starts immediately for the new chapters.

POST /api/novel/event/generateEvents {projectId, novelIds, concurrentCount=5}. cleanNovel runs a worker pool; per chapter it calls universalAi with the 'Event Extraction' prompt (o_prompt.eventExtraction, user-overridable) demanding EXACTLY one pipe-delimited line: | Ch.X title | characters | core event (30-60 chars, action+result) | mainline relation å¼º/ä¸­/å¼±(strong/medium/weak + 3-8 char reason) | info density high/med/low | estimated episode length in seconds | emotion tags (+-joined from a fixed vocabulary: conflict/horror/emotion/twist/climax/flat/comedy/suspense/emotional-breakdown) |. Result saved to o_novel.event with eventState; failures store errorReason and can be retried per-chapter. The event table is the agents' compressed view of the novel (anti context-overflow).

Socket namespace /api/socket/scriptAgent. Decision agent (system = script_agent_decision.md) must: collect project parameters (episode count, minutes per episode, chapter range, screen orientation, style, paywall strategy â€” dialogue word budget auto-computed at 150 chars/min), validate chapter range against get_novel_events, then run a strictly sequential pipeline Stage1 story skeleton â†’ Stage2 adaptation strategy â†’ Stage3 episode scripts. It dispatches â‰¤100-char task briefs (prefixed with a ã€Project Configã€‘ header) to subagent tools run_sub_agent_storySkeleton / _adaptationStrategy / _script and auto-triggers run_supervision_agent after stages 1-2; the supervision report (grade A/B/C/D + issue table with severity/suggestions) is shown and the agent MUST wait for the user's verdict (pass/fix/redo). Stage 3 loops one episode per subagent call, default 3, max 5 per batch, silent during the loop. Read tools: get_novel_events(chapterIndexs[]), get_novel_text(chapterIndex), get_planData(key), get_script_content(ids, last episode only for continuity). Execution agents emit workspace XML (<storySkeleton>â€¦, <adaptationStrategy>â€¦, <scriptItem name="â€¦">â€¦) which the renderer parses out of the stream into workspace panes and persists via /api/scriptAgent/setPlanData (skeleton+strategy JSON into o_agentWorkData; scripts upserted into o_script by name). Subagents must end with a one-line confirmation ('Story skeleton saved, see the workspace on the right') and never recap content; the decision layer may never do execution work itself nor trigger review after a failed subagent.

featureInventory.feature: Persistent agent memory with local embeddings
featureInventory.howItWorks: Every user/assistant turn is embedded with a bundled ONNX all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (fp16, fully offline via @huggingface/transformers) and stored in `memories` per isolationKey. Every 10 messages the agent's own LLM compresses them into a â‰¤500-char summary ('You are a memory compression assistantâ€¦'). Context block assembled per turn: RAG top-3 similar messages + last 10 summaries + last 5 raw messages. deepRetrieve tool: vector-search summaries â†’ LLM relevance filter ('return only relevant summary ids as a JSON array') â†’ expand to original messages. Settings expose all tuning knobs and memory wipe; routes /api/agents/getMemory|clearMemory.

POST /api/script/extractAssets {scriptIds, projectId}: scripts marked extractState=2 (queued), grouped 5 per LLM call; universalAi gets the 'Script Asset Extraction' prompt (o_prompt) + existing asset list and MUST return via a forced resultTool (zod schema): newAssets[{name (exact name only), desc (30-80 char visual description), prompt (English comma-separated image prompt), type role|scene|tool, scriptIds[]}] and existingAssetRefs[{name, scriptIds}]. Persisted into o_assets + o_scriptAssets (old links for those scripts replaced); per-script states polled via /api/script/pollScriptAssets. Extraction rules: skip unnamed extras, dedupe day/night variants of a scene, only plot-relevant props, visual-first descriptions.

featureInventory.feature: Asset library & AI image generation with art-style visual manuals
featureInventory.howItWorks: Assets page lists roles/scenes/props (plus uploaded audio and clip files via /api/assets/uploadClip and /api/assets/addAudioAssets). 'Polish prompt' (/api/assetsGenerate/polishAssetsPrompt, batch variant exists) builds the image prompt by feeding name+describe to universalAi with the style's visual manual: prefix.md (global aesthetic: style genome, 10-color palette with hard/soft constraint tiers, emotion-palette table, color-temperature targets, mandatory rules R1-R5 and forbidden list X1-X6) + art_character.md (or art_scene/art_prop, *_derivative for derived assets). art_character.md mandates a FOUR-VIEW character sheet: portrait closeup (headâ†’collarbone) + front + side + back full-body on one canvas, neutral background with fixed hex, height/head-ratio constraints by gender, no makeup, identity-appropriate base outfit, 'no text in image'. Image generation (/api/assetsGenerate/generateAssets) inserts an o_image placeholder ('ç”Ÿæˆä¸­'), wraps the polished prompt in a fixed Chinese envelope ('Please generate {standard four-view sheet|standard scene image|standard prop image} per the following parametersâ€¦ strictly follow system specs'), calls u.Ai.Image(model).run with optional reference image, saves JPG to OSS /{projectId}/{role|scene|props}/{uuid}.jpg, updates state; frontend polls /api/assets/pollingImageAssets. Multiple historical images per asset; user picks active one (o_assets.imageId).

featureInventory.feature: Art style packs (11 bundled) + custom style extraction
featureInventory.howItWorks: data/skills/art_skills/<style>/ contains README, prefix.md, art_prompt/{art_character, art_character_derivative, art_scene, art_scene_derivative, art_prop, art_prop_derivative, art_storyboard_video}.md, driector_skills/{director_planning_style, director_storyboard, director_storyboard_table_style}.md. Bundled styles: 2D_90s_japanese_anime, 2D_chinese_guofeng, 2D_flat_design, 2D_mature_urban_romance, 3D_anime_render, 3D_chinese_traditional, 3D_clay_stopmotion, 3D_guofeng_cyber, realpeople_ancient_chinese, realpeople_modern_city, realpeople_urban_modern. art_storyboard_video.md is tiny: per-video-mode style tag strings to inject (e.g. EN '90s Japanese anime, hand-drawn cel animation, soft warm tones, cinematicâ€¦' / CN equivalents for Seedance 2.0). Custom styles: /api/artStyle/extractStylePrompt sends reference images to universalAi to produce a one-line bilingual style tag '(ç”»é£Žï¼šâ€¦, english style prompt)'; stored in o_artStyle.

data/skills/story_skills/<genre>/driector_skills/*.md (Comedy_humor, Coming_of_age, Family_warmth, Historical_epic, Horror_supernatural, Hot_blooded_action, Mystery_thriller, Psychological_drama, Scifi_post_apocalypse, Sweet_romance_novel, Urban_workplace_drama, Xianxia_fantasy). Each provides narrative-planning and storyboard-table technique references (e.g. Sweet Romance: restraint & subtext, push-pull rhythm, silence over score, framing/negative-space composition). Loaded as activatable skills for production subagents alongside the art style's director skills.

featureInventory.feature: ProductionAgent â€” six-stage manufacturing pipeline per episode
featureInventory.howItWorks: Socket namespace /api/socket/productionAgent (auth carries projectId+scriptId; updateContext switches episode). Decision agent (production_agent_decision.md) enforces order: 1 director plan â†’ 2 derived-asset analysis â†’ 3 derived-asset image generation (optional, user-confirmed) â†’ 4 storyboard table (ONLY stage with mandatory supervision review) â†’ 5 storyboard panel write â†’ 6 storyboard image generation (skipped logically in multi-ref mode pathway: panel mode chosen by project 'å¤šå‚' flag â€” multi-reference â‡’ 'pure-text multi-param mode' (no prompts, no images, group-per-write, then go to video workbench); else 'first/last-frame mode' (full prompts + images)). Tools per stage: run_sub_agent_director_plan/_derive_assets/_generate_assets/_storyboard_table/_storyboard_panel/_storyboard_gen/_supervision. Global constraints: stages 4-6 may only use existing assets; missing base assets must NEVER be raised as review issues (base assets are pipeline-external input); image generation is async â€” dispatch and tell the user to wait; video generation requests are refused with 'please use the video generation panel'. Each subagent gets the art-style + genre director skills as activatable skills and the model info ('image model X / video model Y / multi-ref yes|no').

production_execution_director_plan.md: persona 'director with 50 years experience'; strictly linear 5 steps; read get_flowData('script') exactly once, then NO more tool calls; faithfully split the script into scenes (cut on location change/time jump/dramatic unit close), per scene count dialogue lines, analyze emotion (intensity + Xâ†’Y arc), list shooting notes (emotional beats, consistency anchors, spatial distances, pitfalls), design inter-scene transitions (only when needed; may invent connective beats â€” the single allowed creative act); FORBIDDEN to plan lighting/color/music (lighting comes from scene images, no BGM in pipeline). Output one-shot <scriptPlan> XML written to the workspace, machine-readable for the storyboard-table stage.

featureInventory.feature: Stage 2/3 â€” Derived assets (visual state variants)
featureInventory.howItWorks: production_execution_derive_assets.md: derived asset = stable, reusable, asset-level visual variant of a parent ('{parent}Â·{state}'), NOT per-shot closeups or transient expressions. Characters: ONLY transformation states (outfit change / transformation VFX / shapeshift e.g. beastform, giant, missing limb); scenes: ONLY time-of-day variants (dayâ†’night/dusk/dawn); props: never. 1-5 per asset, 'better none than noise'. Each new variant must be written via add_deriveAsset({assetsId, id:null, name 2-6 chars, desc '[difference from default] Â· [visual features]' â‰¤100 chars, type}) â€” call count must equal variant count (anti 'analysis without writes'). Stage 3 (production_execution_generate_assets.md) just collects ids and fires generate_assets_images (async). Derived prompt-polish uses art_*_derivative.md manuals which reference the parent image ('reference main view + target time-of-day').

production_execution_storyboard_table.md: rules priority 'dialogue zero-edit > all present characters visible > action/state description only > long-line splitting'. Segments â‰¤15s; dialogue timed at 4 chars/sec; lines >20 chars must be split across consecutive shots cut at semantic pauses (reaction shots allowed with continuing audio); every scripted character must have visual presence in every scene (background/partial/reaction/silhouette); extras get micro-actions, never their own dialogue; character appearance NEVER goes into descriptions (handled by reference images); sound = ambient + SFX only, never BGM; VO/system text must appear verbatim with line-by-line reveal + tick SFX for screen text. A dedicated 'transition continuity' section bans frozen-jump cuts: adjacent fragments must bridge action (end on motion-in-progress), relay emotion (reaction-shot setups), link space/eyeline (empty shots, carried-over sound), or glue audio across the cut. Linear 6 steps: read script/assets/scriptPlan once â†’ align with scriptPlan (emotion + notes, dialogue counts only rough) â†’ externalizable draft (timing, fragment cuts, bridges, in-fragment shot splits, all-present check) â†’ one-shot <storyboardTable> markdown (per scene: '## åœºN: scene ï½œ å‚æ¼”è§’è‰²: â€¦' then '### ç‰‡æ®µN (çº¦Xs)' with referenced asset names+IDs and a table | åºå· | ç”»é¢æè¿° | æ—¶é•¿ | æ™¯åˆ« | è¿é•œ | å°è¯ | éŸ³æ•ˆ | i.e. #|picture description|duration|shot size|camera move|dialogue|SFX) â†’ red-line self-check (no lighting/color words, no fabricated assets/IDs) â†’ one-line confirmation. Auto-reviewed by the supervision agent.

featureInventory.feature: Stage 5 â€” Storyboard panel write (two modes)
featureInventory.howItWorks: production_execution_storyboard_panel.md: router â€” decision layer specifies the mode. Flow A 'pure-text multi-param' (multi-reference video models): no techniques loaded, no prompts, no images; one add_flowData_storyboard call per GROUP of the table; videoDesc = optional carry-over sentence describing the previous group's freeze-frame end-state (position/pose/facing, same scene only) + the group's table rows VERBATIM; track sequentially numbered across scenes; duration = group duration; associateAssetsIds = the scene's asset-ID list; shouldGenerateImage='false'. Flow C 'first/last-frame': activates storyboard_prompt_techniques + style director_storyboard skills; builds a global character position/facing baseline table (9-cell grid left/center/right Ã— front/mid/back, locked per scene, change-points marked); one call per ROW; generates @å›¾N reference-image labels in asset order, prompt body must use @å›¾N instead of names; videoDesc = structured aggregation of the row's 13 fields; prompt generated per the techniques file then verified field-by-field against the table (6 fidelity checks incl. no lighting words, shot size match, facing match); shouldGenerateImage='true'. Hard constraints: write-count == unit count, durations identical to table, no lighting/color/music words anywhere (they'd fight the scene-image lighting), never call generate_storyboard_images in this stage. Server tool inserts o_storyboard rows + per-storyboard o_videoTrack and notifies canvas via socket.

production_skills/storyboard_prompt_techniques.md: prompt generation is FORMAT CONVERSION not creative writing; the table row is the single source of truth ('picture description is the trunk, style words are decoration'); 'first-frame principle' â€” the storyboard image is the video's first frame: static moments rendered as-is, continuous actions frozen at start instant (sword raised, about to strike), camera moves take the STARTING shot size, fades noted as opening state; fieldâ†’prompt mapping table (camera move and dialogue/SFX never enter image prompts); shot-size vocabulary for two model families (Mode A Seedream: Chinese phrases; Mode B Nanobanana/Gemini: English tags + JSON prompt); per-row post-generation checklist. Style-specific director_storyboard.md supplies emotionâ†’face/eye word maps, time-of-day light vocab, per-scene-type texture words, and mandatory style anchor words.

featureInventory.feature: Stage 6 â€” Storyboard image generation (batch, concurrent, reference-driven)
featureInventory.howItWorks: Agent tool generate_storyboard â†’ renderer â†’ POST /api/production/storyboard/batchGenerateImage {storyboardIds, projectId, scriptId, concurrentCount=5, compulsory}. Marks rows ç”Ÿæˆä¸­ (or æœªç”Ÿæˆ if shouldGenerateImage=0 unless compulsory), responds immediately with the work list, then for each shot builds referenceList = the ordered o_assets2Storyboard asset images as base64 (order defines @å›¾N), calls u.Ai.Image(project.imageModel).run({prompt, size:imageQuality, aspectRatio:videoRatio, referenceList}) in batches of 5, saves to OSS /{projectId}/assets/{scriptId}/{uuid}.jpg, updates state/filePath/reason. Frontend polls /api/production/storyboard/pollingImage. /previewImage composites selected frames into a numbered grid (sharp, max 5 columns, 512px thumbs, index badges) for quick review/export; individual frames replaceable via updateStoryboardUrl/uploadImage.

featureInventory.feature: Video workbench (tracks â†’ prompt â†’ generation â†’ pick)
featureInventory.howItWorks: Per episode. getGenerateData returns tracks: in multi-ref mode each storyboard group is a track whose medias = storyboard frame(s) + the deduped asset images of its shots + bound voice samples (capped by the model's audioReference:N); in frame mode tracks map 1:1 to shots. addTrack/deleteTrack manage segments (trackId = Date.now()). 'Generate prompt' â†’ POST /api/production/workbench/generateVideoPrompt: gathers the track's storyboard rows (videoDesc, duration) + asset roster '[id,type,name]', picks the system prompt by precedence (1) o_modelPrompt binding for vendor+model â†’ md file under data/modelPrompt; (2) filename auto-match: wan2.6â†’wan2.6Single-imageFirstFrameMode.md, seedance 2.0â†’seedance2Multi-parameterMode.md, frame modesâ†’universalFirstAndLastFrameMode.md, JSON-array modeâ†’universalMulti-parameterMode.md; (3) fallback to o_prompt.videoPromptGeneration; prepends the style's art_storyboard_video.md tags as an assistant message; universalAi returns the final video prompt stored on o_videoTrack (editable via updateVideoPrompt, validated via checkVideoPrompt). 'Generate video' â†’ POST generateVideo: resolves media to base64, inserts o_video (ç”Ÿæˆä¸­), responds with videoId, then u.Ai.Video(model).run({prompt, referenceList, mode, duration, aspectRatio, resolution, audio}) â†’ vendor adapter (e.g. volcengine: content array with first_frame/last_frame roles or reference_image/video/audio lists; provider job polled by pollTask) â†’ save mp4 to OSS â†’ state ç”ŸæˆæˆåŠŸ/ç”Ÿæˆå¤±è´¥. Frontend polls checkVideoStateList; multiple candidates per track; selectVideo marks the keeper; updateVideoDuration trims; batchGeneratePrompt/batchGenerateVideo loop tracks. Final assembly/stitching/export happens in the web frontend (no ffmpeg server-side).

User uploads named/described voice samples as audio assets. POST /api/cornerScape/batchBindAudio: for each role asset, universalAi with the 'voice matching' prompt (match by gender/age/personality semantics; one voice per role; omit if nothing fits) + audio list 'ID|name|description' must call a resultTool({audioId}) â†’ o_assetsRole2Audio upsert; state on o_assets.audioBindState; polled via pollingAudio. Bound voices flow into multi-ref video tracks as audio references (lip-sync/voice cloning for Seedance-2.0-style models). A 'ttsDubbing' agent slot exists but is disabled by default.

A canvas (saved in o_imageFlow as {nodes, edges}) for fine-tuning storyboard frames: 'upload' nodes hold images, 'generated' nodes hold a prompt + reference images (edges define which upstream images feed it). POST /api/production/editImage/generateFlowImage runs u.Ai.Image with the references and saves to /{projectId}/workFlow/; results can be written back to assets/storyboards (updateAssetsUrl/updateStoryboardUrl). README calls this 'node-based refinement that flows back to the workbench'.

Every model invocation wrapped in withTaskRecord lands in o_tasks; /api/task/getTaskApi paginates with filters (state, taskClass, projectId, joined to project), /getTaskCategories lists classes, /taskDetails shows payload+reason. Pure observability â€” no retry/cancel from here (cancelGenerate exists only for asset generation).

Sections (src/routes/setting/*): vendorConfig â€” enable vendors, edit inputValues, ADD/EDIT vendor TypeScript code in-app (updateCode writes data/vendor/<id>.ts, getCodeByLink imports community vendors by URL), manage extra models, live model tests for text/image/video; agentDeploy â€” simple/advanced mode toggle (agentUseMode), assign vendor+model per agent or per subagent with temperature/maxTokens; promptManage â€” edit the four o_prompt templates (useData override); modelMap â€” bind prompt md files to image/video models, CRUD prompt files; skillManagement â€” list/read/save all skill md files; memoryConfig â€” view/confirm/clear memories and tune summary/RAG knobs; dbConfig â€” DB info, export/import full data zip, clear tables; loginConfig â€” change password; dev â€” toggle AI SDK devtools; fileManagement â€” open data folder; about â€” version check/update download.

/api/script/exportScript streams selected episodes as a zip of .txt files (compressing lib). /api/general/generalStatistics powers a home dashboard; getSingleProject/updateProject manage project settings. Login page (JWT) guards everything.

Frameless window; renderer calls toonflow:// pseudo-protocol for window controls/devtools/external links/locale; data seeded into userData on version change; native modules loaded from app.asar.unpacked. For a web rebuild: replace with normal browser chrome â€” keep the random-port + getappurl pattern only if shipping desktop.

aiIntegration:
PROVIDERS & WIRING
- Vercel AI SDK v6 ('ai' package) for all text/LLM calls; provider factories injected into the vendor sandbox: @ai-sdk/openai, @ai-sdk/anthropic, @ai-sdk/google, @ai-sdk/deepseek, @ai-sdk/xai, @ai-sdk/openai-compatible, zhipu-ai-provider, qwen-ai-provider-v5, vercel-minimax-ai-provider. Image/video/TTS are raw HTTP in vendor code (e.g. Volcengine Ark /images/generations and async video jobs polled via pollTask; Kling, Vidu, MiniMax, GRS AI, 'toonflow' first-party relay). A vendor's textRequest(model, think, thinkLevel) returns an AI-SDK chat model â€” e.g. volcengine wraps createOpenAICompatible with a custom fetch that injects {thinking:{type:'enabled'}, reasoning_effort: minimal|low|medium|high} from thinkLevel 0-3. Reasoning streams surface either as AI-SDK reasoning chunks or inline <think>/<reasoning_content> tags (extractReasoningMiddleware + AutoThinkingTextStream both handle it).
- Model identity everywhere is the string 'vendorId:modelName'. Agent roles resolve through o_agentDeploy with simple/advanced modes. Tool-call loop capped at stepCountIs(50Ã—toolCount).
- Video model capability metadata drives UX: per-model mode (text | singleImage | startEndRequired | endFrameOptional | startFrameOptional | array of imageReference:N/videoReference:N/audioReference:N), audio flag, durationResolutionMap. The project stores the chosen mode; Array.isArray(mode) â‡’ 'multi-reference' pipeline branch.
- Orchestration: no queue framework; immediate HTTP ACK + background async with DB state machines + client polling endpoints; hand-rolled concurrency pools (default 5) for event extraction, asset image gen, storyboard image gen; an 800 ms serialized socket queue for agentâ†’renderer commands; pollTask(interval 3 s, timeout ~50 min) inside vendors for provider job polling; urlToBase64 3 retries; every generation logged to o_tasks with success/fail+reason.

PROMPT TEMPLATES (all originally Chinese; structure & intent translated)
1) Event extraction (o_prompt.eventExtraction): 'You are a novel text-analysis assistant. The user supplies one chapter; output EXACTLY one line, starting and ending with |, with exactly 7 fields: | Ch.X {title} | {characters with real scenes} | {core event, 30-60 chars, must contain action+result} | {mainline relation: strong/medium/weak (3-8 char reason)} | {info density high/med/low} | {estimated episode length, seconds only} | {emotion tags joined by +, from: conflict, horror, emotion, twist, climax, flat, comedy, suspense, emotional breakdown} |. No preamble, no table header, no markdown, no explanation. Be faithful to the text; pick the plotline most affecting the protagonist; for dialogue-heavy chapters report what the dialogue achieved.' Includes worked examples and duration guidance (high density+emotion â†’ 45-60 s, mid â†’ 35-45 s, low â†’ 25-35 s).
2) Script asset extraction (o_prompt.scriptAssetExtraction): system persona 'professional script-content analysis assistant'; must return via resultTool (schema-validated); per asset {name = exact script appellation, desc = 30-80 char visual description, prompt = English comma-separated visual keywords incl. style words, type role|scene|tool}; rules per type (characters: appearance/outfit/build; scenes: spatial structure/lighting/key furnishings/palette, don't duplicate day/night; props: only those with independent visual or plot value); principles: faithful to script, visual-first, lean extraction, accurate classification, executable English prompts. Caller appends: multi-script batch note (scripts delimited by '===== ã€å‰§æœ¬ID: xã€‘ =====', tag each asset with scriptIds) and the existing-asset reuse hint.
3) Video prompt generation (o_prompt.videoPromptGeneration + data/modelPrompt/video/*.md): a router skill. Mode routing: model name Seedance2.0 â‡’ Seedance mode; Wan2.6 â‡’ Wan mode; otherwise multi-ref flag yes â‡’ 'universal multi-parameter', no â‡’ 'universal first/last-frame'. Inputs: model name, asset roster '[id, type, name]', and <storyboardItem videoDesc='â€¦' duration='â€¦'> list; videoDesc parsed into 12 fields (picture description, scene, asset names, duration, shot size, camera move, character action, emotion, light/mood, dialogue, SFX, asset IDs). Unified @å›¾N numbering: assets first in input order, then storyboard frames (skipping shouldGenerateImage=false). Output is ONE consolidated prompt, nothing else. â€¢ Universal multi-param: '[References]' list mapping @å›¾N to '[{name} reference image]'/'[storyboard N]', then '[Instruction]' in English: 'Based on the storyboard @å›¾4: @å›¾1 {action}, @å›¾2 {action}, set in the {scene} of @å›¾3, {camera}, {emotional tone}, {dialogue with (dialogue)/(inner monologue, OS)/(voiceover, VO) markers, original language preserved}, {SFX}.' Standard camera tags (cinematic/wide-angle/close-up/slow motion/surround/handheld) and spatial verbs (wearing/holding/standing on/following behind/sitting in); no appearance descriptions (reference images own identity); no durations. â€¢ Universal first/last-frame: pure text, NO @å›¾N; five sections [Visual] (each subject: brief look, blocking, speaking/silent flag) / [Motion] (timeline segments â‰¥1 s, '0s-4s: â€¦') / [Camera] (single continuous take, never cut) / [Audio] (timed lines with speaker + type + lip-sync state; non-speakers marked silent) / [Narrative]; English; dialogue verbatim in original language. â€¢ Seedance 2.0: Chinese; header 'ç”»é¢é£Žæ ¼å’Œç±»åž‹: â€¦' then 'ç”Ÿæˆä¸€ä¸ªç”±ä»¥ä¸‹ N ä¸ªåˆ†é•œç»„æˆçš„è§†é¢‘' with global transition note and per-shot 'åˆ†é•œN<duration-ms>4000</duration-ms>: æ—¶é—´ï¼šduskï¼Œåœºæ™¯å›¾ç‰‡ï¼š@å›¾3 ï¼Œé•œå¤´ï¼šshot size/angle/moveï¼Œ@å›¾1 {action/expression/gaze/blocking}ã€‚â€¦' Dialogue format '@å›¾N è¯´ï¼šã€Œâ€¦ã€éŸ³è‰²ï¼š{9-dimension voice spec: gender, age timbre, pitch, texture, thickness, articulation, breath, speed, special quality}' with a default-voice lookup table by archetype (authoritative male, gentle female, young male, lively female, cold villain); inner monologue 'å†…å¿ƒOSï¼š' (mouth closed), voiceover 'ç”»å¤–éŸ³VOï¼š'; min 1000 ms per shot; secondsÃ—1000 into duration-ms. â€¢ Wan 2.6: single shot in, single English narrative paragraph out (novel-like, no tag dumps, no @å›¾N): style sentence â†’ subject+action with looks embedded, emotion shown through action â†’ environment woven into narrative â†’ standalone light sentence â†’ dialogue line (original language, typed) â†’ SFX â†’ camera sentence. Shared constraints: strictly follow videoDesc, dialogue never omitted/translated, dialogue type always marked, â‰¥1 s granularity, visual style comes from the art style's art_storyboard_video.md ('Visual style constraints' assistant message), output prompt text only.
4) Voice binding (o_prompt.audioBindPrompt): 'You are a voice-matching assistant; given a role's name+description and a candidate audio list, pick the best voice by gender/age/personality semantics; one voice per role; return nothing if no fit' + forced resultTool({audioId}).
5) AI chapter-regex: 'You are a regex expert; given script text, return one JS regex /â€¦/g with two capture groups (episode number incl. Chinese numerals, episode title); regex only, empty string if no pattern.'
6) Style extraction from images: returns exactly '(ç”»é£Žï¼š{Chinese}, {idiomatic English style prompt})' or 'æ— æ³•æè¿°' (indescribable), one combined tag for multiple images.
7) ScriptAgent skills (data/skills/script_*.md): decision (parameter intake/validation, dispatch templates, A-D review-grade handling, hard bans on self-execution and reviewing failed work); skeleton execution â€” a full short-drama writing-room playbook: three core principles (short drama = instant-emotion product for paid traffic; three densities â€” emotion/information/plot â€” as the master yardstick; expectation management buildâ†’breakâ†’replant), big-triangle/small-triangle conflict structure with a 4-level conflict ladder ('the best conflict is two good people driven to different fates'), psychological satisfaction types (advantage/golden-finger, belonging, order) + originality rule (any trope seen >10Ã— in market is banned), character dossiers â‰¤4 leads (5 elements + relatability five + duality + golden-finger boundaries + speech style + entrance technique), first-10-episodes golden structure ('3 episodes decide life or death'), paywall points at 10/30/50/70/90% with 5 standards and 4 archetypes, genre rhythm frameworks (sweet-pet, tortured-love, cute-baby, war-god, rebirth), emotion-arc tables, info-gap design, episode-end hook taxonomy, ~3 'stock-price-level' twists registered in a ledger (misdirection / persona-flip(only side characters) / motive-swap); output skeleton inside <storySkeleton> with fixed sections (story core, hidden arc, dossiers, three acts, per-episode decisions in mode A â‰¤20 eps or mode B overview table with 'one row = one episode' hard rules, deletion ledger, paywall design, twist ledger) + internal self-check list; adaptation execution â€” 8 adaptation principles (filmability, lean dialogue, extreme pace, single mainline, low comprehension cost, emotion over logic, opening expectation, show-don't-tell) + innovation directions, output <adaptationStrategy>; script execution â€” per-episode screenplay with beat rules (every episode needs burst/torture/satisfaction point; satisfaction formula = disguise + face-slap + shock + reward; 3-15-45 second rhythm; 4 emotion channels; dialogue â‰¤20 chars/line, â‰¤50 chars/turn; five 'visual writing' moves; five rookie flaws to avoid), strict output format: <scriptItem name='{Work} EP{NN}: {title}'> â†’ header (target minutes â‰ˆ chars, platform/style/beats) â†’ 200-300 char synopsis â†’ scenes '1-1 {scene} æ—¥/å†…', 'äººç‰©: â€¦' cast line, â–³ action/description lines (write HOW, filmable, AI-ready), '{Name}: dialogue', OS/V.S. with emotion, '---' between scenes, transition marks [ç¡¬åˆ‡ hard cut/æ·¡å…¥ fade in/é—ªç™½ flash white/é—ªé»‘ flash black/å åŒ– dissolve]; banned outputs (word counts, version tags, beat timings, camera-tech brackets, any meta); supervision â€” grades A-D, severity table ðŸ”´/ðŸŸ¡/âšª with fix options, 'only raise issues, user decides'.
8) ProductionAgent skills (production_*.md + production_skills/*) as detailed in the feature inventory (director plan, derive assets, storyboard table, panel modes, prompt techniques).
9) Art-style manuals (prefix + per-asset-type templates with rule/forbidden tables and a fill-in prompt template; e.g. four-view sheet spec) and genre director packs â€” these are the 'visual manual' system prompts for asset prompt polishing and storyboard prompt generation.
10) Memory summarizer: 'compress these memories into one summary â‰¤N chars, output summary only'; memory relevance judge: 'given keyword + summaries, return JSON array of relevant ids only'.
uiPatterns:
(Frontend source is the separate Toonflow-web repo; patterns reconstructed from the API/socket contracts, README screenshots and seeded data.)
- Shell: frameless desktop window with custom min/max/close buttons (toonflow:// protocol), login screen (admin/admin123), left nav: Home/dashboard (generalStatistics), Projects, Task Center, Settings. 7 locales, OS-locale autodetect.
- Project wizard: name/synopsis/genre, art-style picker (cards with style README/images from /skills static mount), story-genre 'director manual' picker, aspect ratio 16:9/9:16, image model + quality (1K/2K/4K), video model + mode picker (modes come from vendor model metadata; choosing a multi-reference mode flips the whole production flow), all model dropdowns fed by /api/modelSelect/getModelList grouped by vendor.
- Novel workspace: chapter list with volume/reel grouping, paste-import with AI-detected chapter regex (editable), per-chapter event extraction status chips (pending/ok/failed with reason tooltip + retry), event text inline.
- ScriptAgent screen = split view: LEFT chat panel (streaming bubbles with role badges named like film crew â€” ç»Ÿç­¹ 'coordinator' for script decision, ç¼–å‰§ 'screenwriter', ç¼–è¾‘ 'editor'; collapsible thinking sections with 'æ€è€ƒä¸­â€¦/Thought for X s' titles; tool activity rendered as expanding thinking blocks; think-level toggle 0-3; stop button) â€” RIGHT workspace tabs: Story Skeleton (markdown), Adaptation Strategy (markdown), Scripts (episode list, editable, exportable as zip). The client parses <storySkeleton>/<adaptationStrategy>/<scriptItem> XML out of the agent stream into these panes and persists via REST (getPlanData socket ack serves the agent's reads from the SAME pane state â€” the workspace is the shared blackboard between user and agent).
- Script manager: episode CRUD, batch add, per-episode 'extract assets' with state chips, deletion guarded ('delete in the script manager manually' â€” agents refuse).
- Asset center: tabs for roles/scenes/props/audio/clips; cards with image, name, description, prompt; 'polish prompt' and 'generate image' buttons (single + batch with concurrency), state badges polled; image history per asset with active-image selection; audio upload with name/description and AI 'match voices to roles' batch action; derived assets nest under parents.
- ProductionAgent screen = the 'infinite canvas workbench' (README's headline feature): free-form canvas organizing nodes for script, director plan, assets (with derive children), storyboard table (markdown) and the storyboard panel â€” a card grid where each shot card shows frame image (or 'no image' in multi-ref mode), videoDesc, duration, track/group label, linked asset chips, state badge, regenerate button; drag to reorder (persisted via saveFlowData â†’ o_storyboard.index); chat docked beside it (roles: è§†é¢‘ç­–åˆ’ 'video producer/planner', æ‰§è¡Œå¯¼æ¼” 'executive director', ç›‘åˆ¶ 'supervising producer'). Storyboard preview = server-composited numbered contact sheet.
- Video workbench: track timeline; each track shows its medias (storyboard frames + asset reference images + voice chips), the generated video prompt (editable textarea with 'check prompt' validation), generate button, then a candidate strip of generated videos (poll-refreshed) with pick-one selection, duration trim, per-track regenerate; batch generate prompts/videos; final stitch/export of selected candidates happens client-side; 'agent refuses video generation in chat and points user here'.
- Node image editor: React-Flow-style graph (upload nodes, generated nodes with reference edges), per-node prompt + model/quality/ratio, results written back to storyboard/assets.
- Task center: filterable table (class/state/project) with model, description, payload JSON, failure reason.
- Settings center: vendor cards with enable toggles, credential inputs (schema-driven from vendor 'inputs'), Monaco editor for vendor TypeScript (monaco workers ship in data/web), 'test model' dialogs for text/image/video; agent deploy matrix (simple mode: 4 agents; advanced mode: 16 subagent rows) with model dropdowns + temperature/maxTokens; prompt manager (default vs override); model-prompt binding manager; skill file browser/editor; memory browser with knobs; DB export/import; about/update.
pitfalls:
- No real job system: every long generation is fire-and-forget after the HTTP ACK; a server restart strands rows in 'ç”Ÿæˆä¸­' forever (no resume/timeout sweeper); rebuild with a persistent queue (e.g. DB-backed jobs + worker) and idempotent state transitions.
- Status machines are Chinese string literals scattered across tables ('ç”Ÿæˆä¸­'/'å·²å®Œæˆ' vs 'ç”ŸæˆæˆåŠŸ' for videos â€” inconsistent vocabulary per table) plus parallel int enums (eventState, extractState). Centralize as typed enums.
- Heavy client-polling (8+ bespoke polling endpoints) despite Socket.IO already being connected â€” push generation state over the socket instead.
- Agent tools depend on the RENDERER as the source of truth (get_flowData/get_planData are socket round-trips to the browser; addStoryboard goes agentâ†’serverâ†’browserâ†’RESTâ†’DB). If the tab disconnects mid-run, tool calls hang or silently no-op (promises without timeouts). Rebuild with server-owned workspace state; let the client be a subscriber.
- Workspace artifacts ride inside the LLM text stream as XML (<scriptItem> etc.) parsed by the frontend; prompts beg the model to 'output the tag exactly once, never split' â€” fragile. Prefer tool calls/structured outputs for artifact writes (the panel stage already proves this works with add_flowData_storyboard).
- Security: plaintext passwords; 8-char JWT secret; vendor code is arbitrary TS executed in vm2 â€” vm2 is DEPRECATED with known sandbox escapes, and the sandbox hands it axios/fetch/crypto; CORS '*'; tokens accepted via query string. A web multi-tenant rebuild must isolate vendor code (separate process/worker, allowlisted fetch) and hash passwords.
- All media moves as base64 through memory (urlToBase64 even for FULL VIDEOS; referenceList base64 arrays; 100 MB JSON body limit) â€” use streams/presigned URLs.
- IDs from Date.now() (o_project.id, o_videoTrack.id, addStoryboard trackId) â€” collision-prone; addStoryboard creates a videoTrack per shot even when unused.
- Race/async bugs to not copy: generateAssets calls aiImage.save() WITHOUT await then immediately reads/updates the o_image row; AiAudio.run swallows all errors (empty catch); extractAssets chunkArray hardcodes inner chunk size 5 and ignores groupSize semantics; o_agentWorkData queried with String(projectId) vs numeric inserts (SQLite leniency masks it); fixDB-style ad-hoc migrations instead of versioned ones.
- Dead/duplicated code: src/socket/'resTool copy.ts', commented-out run_sub_agent_execution, consumeFullStream duplicated in both agents, two pollingImage routes with near-identical logic. Typos baked into the data model and APIs: 'driector_skills' folder name, thinlLevel, toolCpnfig, 'é¦–ä½å¸§' (should be é¦–å°¾å¸§ first/last frame) â€” if you keep skill folders compatible you must keep the typo or migrate.
- Per-call DB reads of settings/prompts (no caching) and synchronous knex-on-better-sqlite3 in the request path â€” fine for desktop, needs pooling/caching for web.
- The supervision layer reviews only stages 1/2 (script) and stage 4 (production); review reports are markdown conventions, not structured data â€” consider structured review results for actionable UI.
- i18n: backend messages, DB state values and ALL prompts are Chinese; a multi-locale rebuild needs a separation between machine states, prompt language and UI language from day one.
- The frontend repo is separate (Toonflow-web); this repo only ships its build output â€” UI internals (canvas implementation, XML stream parsing, stitching/export) must be re-specified, not copied.
